title: Fix Stored XSS in Migration Scorecard Record Fields
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — High Severity**

Multiple fields in the Migration Scorecard Record were vulnerable to Stored XSS: Notes, Source Object, Priority, Module, and Metrics. Any user with migration editing permissions could inject an XSS payload (e.g., `![](x)`) into these fields, causing arbitrary JavaScript execution for all users visiting the migration endpoint.

## Affected Endpoint

`/dashboard?view=migration`

## Risk

High — attacker could execute arbitrary JavaScript, exfiltrate CSRF tokens. Session cookies protected by HTTPOnly attribute.

## Fix

Input validation applied to all affected Migration Scorecard fields to strip or encode malicious content.
acceptance_criteria: Notes, Source Object, Priority, Module, and Metrics fields sanitize HTML/script content on save
XSS payloads injected through migration record editing are neutralized
Legitimate text in affected fields is preserved without corruption
Migration page renders scorecard records safely
middleware_changes: Input sanitization applied to Migration Scorecard update controller for Notes, Source Object, Priority, Module, and Metrics fields.
implementation_notes: Part of the broader penetration test remediation effort. Same sanitization pattern used across all editable fields.
comment_summary: Developer fixed and pushed to staging for review.
